message:
feat(extensions-js): add compat-scan mode and smarter CJS-to-ESM binding injection

Two improvements to the JS extension compatibility layer:

1. Compat-scan mode (PI_EXT_COMPAT_SCAN):
   - parse_truthy_flag() handles 1/true/yes/on case-insensitively
   - is_compat_scan_mode() checks both explicit env map and process env
   - compat_env_fallback_value() returns deterministic placeholder values
     for API key and semantic legacy env vars during conformance scans
   - PiJS runtime env.get handler checks compat fallback before normal
     env lookup, keeping conformance test output reproducible

2. Smarter CJS-to-ESM wrapper (source_declares_binding):
   - New source_declares_binding() scans for existing const/let/var/
     function/class declarations to avoid re-declaring bindings that
     the source already provides (e.g., a module that defines its own
     `require`, `__filename`, `module`, or `exports`)
   - Rename has_exports_binding → has_exports_usage to distinguish
     usage of exports.foo from a binding declaration
   - Selectively inject only needed CJS compat globals: __filename,
     __dirname, module, exports — each independently guarded

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
